what_it_does: Defines an SVG icon showing an outlined circle with an X-shaped error mark inside it.
observations: SVG uses a 24 by 24 viewBox with no fixed width or height.
Rendering is stroke-based with fill set to none.
Stroke color is currentColor, allowing the icon to inherit text or parent color.
The outer shape is a circle centered at 12,12 with radius 9.
The inner X is drawn with two diagonal line segments inside a single path element.
Stroke width is 1.5 with round caps and round joins.
standouts: Color inheritance is built in through currentColor.
The icon is composed only from a circle and crossed diagonal strokes.
The file is a compact inline SVG asset.
conventions: Follows common outline-icon conventions: 24x24 viewBox, no fill, currentColor stroke, rounded line caps and joins.
Uses semantic filename wording that matches the visible error-outline shape.
Keeps all SVG markup on one line.
concerns: No title, desc, role, aria-hidden, or focusable attributes are present, so standalone accessibility behavior is not specified in the file.
The combined X path makes the two diagonal strokes less individually addressable.
notes: A reusable color-inheriting outline error icon made from a circle and an internal X mark.
questions: Should accessibility metadata be supplied here or by the consuming component?
purpose_vs_actual: The apparent purpose matches the content: an error-outline SVG icon is represented by an outlined circle with an X mark.
intention_signals: The file seems intended to provide a scalable, themeable error icon that can be colored by its surrounding UI context.
contribution: Contributes a reusable stroke-based error-outline symbol with inherited color behavior and a simple circle-plus-X visual structure for a fused design asset set.
summary_for_embedding: This is a compact 24x24 SVG error-outline icon composed of a currentColor stroked circle and an X-shaped path.
intention_for_embedding: This was for representing an error or failure state in a scalable outline icon style that adapts to surrounding color.
